key_points: Dorm quality varies significantly; older halls have had serious issues
Students have switched rooms multiple times due to bad roommates
Freshmen ask about private rooms and bathroom access before committing
key_quotes: I use to live in Sherwood Halls before it had air conditioning, and also heat.
My brother almost wanted to switch colleges because of the coaches etc. He didn't really like some of his roommates so he switched multiple times.
If you are a first year student/freshman can you pay extra for a private dorm? And if so do they have their own bathrooms?

key_points: Social life is a consistent, repeated complaint from prospective students
Macon location limits off-campus options; party scene is minimal
Small school size means less traditional college experience overall
key_quotes: The downsides of Mercer is the social life, the surrounding areas, the price and Demographics.
Is there like really no opportunity for parties and chilling out? Or is there some you can find if you go out of the campus, like the town?
Small school (6k students), more opportunities to go abroad, less traditional college life, in Macon (scary).

key_points: Meal plans are auto-assigned and may not match student needs
Students with transfer credits got cheaper plans by default
Limited data suggests dining is an afterthought for most applicants
key_quotes: For the meal plans, they auto assigned me to one of the cheaper plans due to me having 33 credit hours so they should allow people to change now.
The school seems amazing and I'm really excited to start.

key_points: Rigorous coursework cited as intimidating by multiple prospective students
No specific mental health resources mentioned by any student reviewer
Pre-med track pressure is real; burnout risk acknowledged by students
key_quotes: The lack of diversity and the vigorous coursework is kinda throwing me off.
This video got me contemplating because Mercer Uni was becoming a top choice for me... these pros and cons have me contemplating.

key_points: Merit scholarships are generous and frequently cited as the top draw
Students receiving $19,000–$20,000 in scholarships report still wondering about financial aid
Full-ride scenarios possible for zero-EFC students with transfer credits
key_quotes: I'm honestly considering Mercer. they GAVE ME $19,000 in academic scholarships and I'm waiting on my financial aid.
My family has an EFC of zero, so I get the full [aid].
I was recently accepted into Mercer with a merit scholarship and GA Tech (my top options), so I'm trying to weigh my options.

key_points: Engineering and pre-med programs are the main academic draws
Guaranteed medical school admission is a rare and highly valued feature
Small class sizes seen as a potential advantage for premed students
key_quotes: Mercer University — Stamps Scholarship & GUARANTEED Medical School.
A guaranteed admission to medical school is VERY hard to pass. Take the opportunity unless you are not 100% set on going into medicine.
Hey I'm super late to this train. But I got my degree in Mechanical Engineering at Mercer.

key_points: Internship requirement for graduation enforced without adequate placement support
Marketing student nearly missed graduation over internship requirement
Limited visibility into how admin handles student issues or complaints
key_quotes: I'm a marketing senior at Mercer University. I've been applying like crazy to jobs and internships with no such luck. I'm now about a week from my graduation ceremony, and it seems like I'm not going to get my diploma this spring.
I'd strongly advise anyone applying to college to stay away from Mercer University unless they want to be held back a year from getting their diploma because of a damn internship.

key_points: Macon, Georgia described as 'scary' by multiple students and commenters
Campus itself is considered pleasant but surrounding area is a concern
Far from Atlanta; limited urban amenities compared to GSU or Emory
key_quotes: Small school (6k students), more opportunities to go abroad, less traditional college life, in Macon (scary).
The downsides of Mercer is the social life, the surrounding areas, the price and Demographics.
What do you think about the safety?

key_points: Engineering job placement spoken of positively by alumni commenters
Guaranteed med school pipeline is the strongest career outcome feature
Marketing and other non-STEM outcomes unclear; internship issues suggest gaps
key_quotes: They upset Duke a couple years back so they're the best school in America basically. Seriously tho you should definitely consider it for the job placement and solid program.
I'm a marketing senior at Mercer University. I've been applying like crazy to jobs and internships with no such luck.
If you are planning to go into medicine, I would take this money and go straight to the place that gave you direct admission to med school.

key_points: Strong value for pre-med and engineering students with scholarships
At ~$25k/year after aid, competes well against Cornell at $60k or GSU at $20k
Non-STEM students may not see the same return on investment
key_quotes: For Cornell University, I will have to pay around 60k per year, for Mercer about 25k per year and for GSU 20k per year.
I'd say Mercer, I think it is all around the best school for you.
they GAVE ME $19,000 in academic scholarships and I'm waiting on my financial aid. But the downsides of Mercer is the social life, the surrounding areas, the price and Demographics.

key_points: Best fit for serious pre-med and engineering students who prioritize outcomes over fun
Diversity concerns are real and openly discussed by Black students
Students who thrive are self-directed; those wanting traditional college life struggle
key_quotes: I got into Mercer for the freshman class of 2019, but the lack of diversity and the vigorous coursework is kinda throwing me off.
How is it like being a black girl at Mercer? I'm considering going but I don't want to feel out of place.
My father did undergrad and grad school there, and he loved it so much.

key_points: **Administration:** Internship graduation requirement enforced with no real placement help
**Diversity:** Multiple Black students openly question whether they will belong or feel safe
**Location:** Macon repeatedly called 'scary'; safety concerns raised unprompted by students
key_quotes: I'd strongly advise anyone applying to college to stay away from Mercer University unless they want to be held back a year from getting their diploma because of a damn internship.
I've been discriminated against a lot at my current Uni (PWI) and I'm premed. I was looking at Mercer school of medicine but by how everyone was in this video..... nah lol.
The downsides of Mercer is the social life, the surrounding areas, the price and Demographics.

key_points: Guaranteed medical school admission program is genuinely rare nationally
Dual enrollment credits can cut two full years off your degree cost
Stamps Scholarship available here — same prestigious award as Georgia Tech
key_quotes: Mercer University — Stamps Scholarship & GUARANTEED Medical School: Small school (6k students), more opportunities to go abroad.
I took a lot of dual enrollment courses in a nearby state college, and the majority of my credits will transfer to Mercer, so that saves me two years worth of college right off the bat.
Although Stamps at GT is an amazing opportunity, it would be a shame if you gave up a guaranteed medical seat if you know that MD is your path.
